title: Building AI Agent Systems and Scaling Challenges in Agentic AI
summary: Sam Anthony discusses the scaling challenges of agentic AI systems, highlighting how expansion increases cost, latency, and failure risk. The talk covers multi-agent design, coordination strategies, and system architecture as key factors affecting real-world scalability.
writeup: Sam Anthony examines why agentic AI systems become harder to run as they grow, with expansion driving up cost, latency, and failure risk. The talk treats multi-agent design, coordination strategies, and system architecture as the central factors that determine whether these systems scale in real-world deployments. The takeaway: scaling an agent system is an architecture and coordination problem, not just a matter of adding more agents.
